entity.summary: Cryptocurrency derivatives exchange founded in 2014. HEI records BitMEX as an active exchange with event-backed regulatory history, including U.S. enforcement actions, a 2021 CFTC/FinCEN settlement, founder guilty pleas, and a later company plea/fine.
entity.notes: Added as a fresh-discovery event-backed record rather than from the verified-unadded 1000-row backlog. BitMEX remains active, so this is not a shutdown record. Regulatory and criminal descriptions are attributed to official and reporting sources.

events.title: BitMEX agreed to CFTC and FinCEN settlement
events.description: BitMEX agreed to pay up to $100 million to settle civil charges brought by the CFTC and FinCEN related to U.S. customer access, registration, and compliance controls.
events.notes: Civil regulatory settlement; it did not make the exchange dead.

events.title: BitMEX founders pleaded guilty to Bank Secrecy Act violations
events.description: The U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of New York announced that Arthur Hayes and Benjamin Delo pleaded guilty to violating the Bank Secrecy Act by willfully failing to establish, implement, and maintain an AML program at BitMEX.
events.notes: Founder-level criminal resolution. It is recorded separately from company-level events.

events.title: BitMEX fined after company guilty plea
events.description: Reuters and WSJ reported that BitMEX was fined $100 million by a U.S. judge after the company pleaded guilty to a Bank Secrecy Act-related charge over anti-money-laundering failures.
events.notes: Company-level resolution event. It follows earlier civil settlement and founder-level pleas.

events.title: BitMEX verified as active exchange entity
events.description: BitMEX was verified as an active exchange through its official website and public reference material.
events.notes: Fresh-discovery record; no verified-unadded backlog row was consumed.

evidence.title: BitMEX agrees to pay $100 million to settle CFTC and FinCEN charges
Reuters background on BitMEX civil settlement and later penalty
evidence.notes: Reuters report includes background that BitMEX agreed in 2021 to pay up to $100 million to settle civil charges.

evidence.title: Founders Of Cryptocurrency Exchange Plead Guilty To Bank Secrecy Act Violations
evidence.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice / Southern District of New York
evidence.notes: Official DOJ source for Hayes and Delo guilty pleas and BitMEX context.

evidence.title: Trump pardons BitMEX co-founders, White House official says
evidence.notes: Secondary report summarizing the 2022 founder pleas and later pardon context.

evidence.title: BitMEX fined $100 million by US judge for anti-money laundering violations
evidence.notes: Report on company fine and guilty plea context.

evidence.title: Crypto Exchange BitMEX Fined $100 Million for Anti-Money-Laundering Failures
evidence.notes: Independent report on BitMEX fine and company plea context.

evidence.title: BitMEX official website
evidence.notes: Official website used to verify active identity.

evidence.title: BitMEX public reference page
evidence.notes: Temporary secondary reference for founding and historical summary. Replace with stronger historical primary sources if found.
